project.name: ai-companion-cli
project.description: AI Companion CLI is a terminal-first chat application focused on multi-turn conversation, streaming output, tool-call visualization, and file-based local persistence.

nodes.name: chat-controller.ts
nodes.summary: Controller that orchestrates a full chat turn, including persistence, graph execution, tool handling, memory, and emotion updates.

nodes.name: emotion-service.ts
nodes.summary: Emotion-state service that computes state transitions across user and assistant turns.

nodes.name: memory-service.ts
nodes.summary: Memory extraction and persistence service that derives candidate memories and manages stored memory records.

nodes.name: session-store.ts
nodes.summary: Session reconstruction layer that rebuilds UI snapshots from persisted sessions, messages, runs, tools, memory, and emotion state.

nodes.summary: LangGraph-based conversation loop that alternates between the agent and tools and emits normalized runtime events.

layers.name: Project Artifacts
layers.description: Repository-level documentation, diagrams, and build or tooling configuration that describe or shape the project as a whole.

layers.name: CLI and UI
layers.description: The terminal entry point, top-level app flow, input handling, and Ink-rendered interface components.

layers.name: Conversation Engine
layers.description: Runtime orchestration for chat turns, graph execution, session reconstruction, and high-level controller behavior.

layers.name: Infrastructure and Persistence
layers.description: Configuration loading, repositories, and file-backed storage primitives that persist runtime state.

layers.name: Prompts and Providers
layers.description: Prompt loading, built-in templates, provider adapters, and capability metadata for model execution.

layers.name: Tools and Shared Types
layers.description: Built-in runtime tools, shared type contracts, and small utility helpers used across the application.

tour.title: Start with the project story
tour.description: Read the repository-facing docs first to understand the current scope, supported commands, and which architecture notes are authoritative.

tour.title: Follow the terminal bootstrap path
tour.description: Move from the CLI entry point into the top-level Ink app to see how the terminal session boots and hands off into runtime state.

tour.title: Trace input and command routing
tour.description: These app-layer modules split slash commands from normal chat turns and keep the UI snapshot synchronized with streaming work.

tour.title: Inspect the conversation engine
tour.description: The controller, graph loop, and tool registry form the runtime core that persists messages, runs tools, and consumes model events.

tour.title: Understand persistence and reconstruction
tour.description: These files show how sessions, messages, runs, and tool executions are written to disk and rebuilt into UI-ready snapshots.

tour.title: Review memory and emotion behavior
tour.description: Memory extraction, emotion state transitions, and overlay synchronization live here and shape the assistant state carried across turns.

tour.title: Finish with providers and prompts
tour.description: The current runtime is wired around DeepSeek, with prompt loading and provider adapters defining how model calls are configured.
